supply_list.quote_context: Use the MUAC (Mid-Upper Arm Circumference) strap on children age 6 months up to 5 years.
Give ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts) and zinc supplement.
Give ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts) and zinc supplement.
Do a Rapid Diagnostic Test (RDT) for malaria before treating.

variables.quote_context: Ask: Does the child have a cough?
Ask: Does the child have fever now or in the last 3 days?

variables.span: 3 or more loose or watery stools in 24 hours
variables.quote_context: Diarrhoea is 3 or more loose or watery stools in 24 hours.
Is there blood in the stool?
to assess, classify, and treat sick children age 2 months up to 5 years
Chest indrawing is when the lower chest wall goes IN when the child breathes in.
Use the MUAC (Mid-Upper Arm Circumference) strap on children age 6 months up to 5 years.
Also check for swelling of both feet (oedema).
Children without danger signs who have diarrhoea, fever (in a malaria area), or cough with fast breathing may be treated at home.
2 months up to 3 years 1 tablet AL Twice daily 3 days / 6 tablets
3 years up to 5 years 2 tablets AL Twice daily 3 days / 12 tablets

predicates.span: A cough for 14 days or more is a danger sign
predicates.quote_context: A cough for 14 days or more is a danger sign.

predicates.span: Fever for 7 days or more is a danger sign
predicates.quote_context: Fever for 7 days or more is a danger sign.

predicates.span: Diarrhoea for 14 days or more is a danger sign
predicates.quote_context: Diarrhoea for 14 days or more is a danger sign.

predicates.span: Blood in stool (dysentery) is a danger sign
predicates.quote_context: Blood in stool (dysentery) is a danger sign.

predicates.span: A convulsion during the current illness is a danger sign
predicates.quote_context: A convulsion during the current illness is a danger sign.

predicates.span: 50 breaths per minute or more
predicates.quote_context: Fast breathing if 2 months up to 12 months 50 breaths per minute or more

predicates.span: 40 breaths per minute or more
predicates.quote_context: Fast breathing if 12 months up to 5 years 40 breaths per minute or more

predicates.span: This is a danger sign indicating severe pneumonia
predicates.quote_context: This is a danger sign indicating severe pneumonia.

predicates.span: vomits everything and cannot hold anything down
predicates.quote_context: A child who vomits everything and cannot hold anything down has a danger sign.

predicates.span: cannot drink or feed anything at all
predicates.quote_context: A child who cannot drink or feed anything at all has a danger sign.

predicates.span: unusually sleepy, difficult to wake, or unconscious
predicates.quote_context: A child who is unusually sleepy, difficult to wake, or unconscious has a danger sign.

predicates.span: If 14 days or more, refer
predicates.quote_context: If yes and for less than 14 days, continue ORS and zinc. If 14 days or more, refer.

predicates.span: dents remain on BOTH feet
predicates.quote_context: If dents remain on BOTH feet, the child has severe malnutrition.

predicates.span: severe malnutrition
predicates.quote_context: If dents remain on BOTH feet, the child has severe malnutrition.

predicates.span: Refer urgently
predicates.quote_context: If dents remain on BOTH feet, the child has severe malnutrition. Refer urgently.

predicates.span: ANY of these danger signs are present
predicates.quote_context: If ANY of these danger signs are present, refer the child urgently.

predicates.span: Give the first dose of medicine before referral if the child can drink
predicates.quote_context: Give the first dose of medicine before referral if the child can drink.

predicates.span: referral note
predicates.quote_context: Write a referral note.

predicates.span: still fast breathing
predicates.quote_context: If still fast breathing, refer.

predicates.span: complete the full 5-day course of amoxicillin
predicates.quote_context: If breathing is normal and child is improving, complete the full 5-day course of amoxicillin.

predicates.span: RDT is positive
predicates.quote_context: Do not give antimalarial medicine unless the RDT is positive.

predicates.span: 1 tablet AL Twice daily 3 days / 6 tablets
predicates.quote_context: 2 months up to 3 years 1 tablet AL Twice daily 3 days / 6 tablets

predicates.span: 2 tablets AL Twice daily 3 days / 12 tablets
predicates.quote_context: 3 years up to 5 years 2 tablets AL Twice daily 3 days / 12 tablets

predicates.span: Give first dose immediately. Second dose 8 hours later. Then morning and evening for 2 more days.
predicates.quote_context: Give first dose immediately. Second dose 8 hours later. Then morning and evening for 2 more days.

predicates.span: Crush tablet and mix with breast milk, water, or food.
predicates.quote_context: Crush tablet and mix with breast milk, water, or food.

predicates.span: If the child spits out the entire dose, give another.
predicates.quote_context: If the child spits out the entire dose, give another.

predicates.span: If unable to take medicine, refer to health facility.
predicates.quote_context: If unable to take medicine, refer to health facility.

predicates.span: RDT is negative
predicates.quote_context: If RDT is negative: do not give antimalarial.

predicates.span: Follow up in 3 days
predicates.quote_context: If RDT is negative: do not give antimalarial. Follow up in 3 days.

predicates.span: If fever persists at follow-up, refer to health facility.
predicates.quote_context: If fever persists at follow-up, refer to health facility.
